methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $120M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $300M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Cemvita Factory is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Moji Karimi with a current post-money valuation of $120M and $30M raised to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five private peers at the same stage—Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.—alongside one relevant public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x applied to the $120M last post-money, implies a valuation of $228M. Peer-median analysis across the five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $400M, with a range of $300M–$5.0B reflecting material dispersion driven partly by Commonwealth Fusion Systems' $5.0B valuation outlier. The median is anchored by Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M).

Triangulating both methods produces a median valuation of $314M with a low estimate of $194M and a high estimate of $460M. The wide range reflects genuine uncertainty around market sentiment for early-stage climate tech at the Series B stage, particularly given the composition of the peer set. Key sensitivities include further progression in the company's technical or commercial milestones relative to peers, and market-level repricing of the climate tech sector. The $314M median implies approximately 2.6x the current valuation.
